sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: The targeted protein degradation landscape advanced significantly in June 2026, marked by major strategic moves from Roche, Arvinas, and Amphista Therapeutics alongside broader regulatory shifts impacting the biotechnology research environment. Roche committed $700 million upfront to collaborate with Nurix Therapeutics on bexobrutideg, a Bruton’s tyrosine kinase degrader for chronic lymphocytic leukemia, signaling confidence in degradation technology over traditional inhibition. Simultaneously, Arvinas expanded its clinical footprint by joining The Michael J. Fox Foundation’s programs to advance ARV-102, an LRRK2-targeting PROTAC for Parkinson’s disease, while finalizing a transaction with Rigel Pharmaceuticals for the exclusive global rights to its approved PROTAC, vepdegestrant. Amphista Therapeutics received FDA clearance for its IND application for AMX-883, a non-cereblon BRD9 degrader utilizing proprietary Targeted Glue™ technology for acute myeloid leukemia. Genovyn bolstered its AI-enabled discovery platform with new leadership appointments. These developments occur amidst significant regulatory changes, including HHS Schedule F designations impacting NIH and FDA oversight, and FDA town halls regarding the National Priority Voucher program. While some reporting cycles noted an absence of specific PROTAC updates, the convergence of substantial business partnerships, regulatory milestones, and clinical advancements underscores a maturing industry transitioning from experimental modalities to approved therapies across oncology and neurodegeneration, supported by diversified E3 ligase recruitment strategies to overcome resistance.

points.text: Roche — Bexobrutideg — Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia — $700M collaboration deal with Nurix Therapeutics.
Amphista Therapeutics — AMX-883 — Acute Myeloid Leukaemia — FDA IND clearance for Phase 1 trial.
Arvinas — ARV-102 — Parkinson’s Disease — Joined Michael J. Fox Foundation LITE and PPMI programs.
Arvinas — VEPPANU — Breast Cancer — Transaction with Rigel Pharmaceuticals for exclusive global rights.
Genovyn — AI Platform — Therapeutic Discovery — Leadership appointments.
Roche: Strategic partnership to codevelop bexobrutideg for blood cancers.
Amphista Therapeutics: Developing non-cereblon Targeted Glue™ degraders for AML.
Arvinas: Clinical-stage biotechnology creating PROTAC therapies for neurodegenerative and oncology indications.
Genovyn: AI-enabled biotechnology accelerating therapeutic discovery and development.
Bexobrutideg: Degrades BTK, Phase 3 study in CLL expected to begin summer 2026.
AMX-883: DCAF16-dependent degrader of BRD9, Phase 1 monotherapy dose-escalation in relapsed/refractory AML.
ARV-102: Targets LRRK2 for neurodegenerative disorders, progressing through clinical development.
VEPPANU: FDA-approved PROTAC for ER+/HER2- ESR1-mutated advanced breast cancer.
Roche and Nurix Therapeutics: $700 million upfront collaboration, up to $2.3 billion potential.
Arvinas and Rigel Pharmaceuticals: Transaction for exclusive global rights of VEPPANU.
Genovyn: Leadership appointments (Sammy Bell as CSO, Jim Edinger as CDO).
